# newswatch
> RSS news monitoring pipeline — collect, extract, semantic search, change track. Runs locally, no API keys.
## Install
```
pip install newswatch          # Python >=3.11
pip install newswatch[dev]     # + pytest, ruff
```
Deps: feedkit, markgrab, embgrep, diffgrab, click, rich
## CLI
```bash
newswatch setup -c technology -c science    # subscribe catalog feeds (-f URL for custom)
newswatch run -n 100 -t https://a.com       # full pipeline, track pages
newswatch search "transformer architecture" -n 10
newswatch --version
```
## API
### PipelineResult (dataclass)
| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `feeds_collected` | `int` | `0` | Feeds polled |
| `articles_new` | `int` | `0` | New articles found |
| `articles_extracted` | `int` | `0` | Articles content-extracted |
| `articles_indexed` | `int` | `0` | Articles added to index |
| `changes_detected` | `int` | `0` | Tracked pages with changes |
| `errors` | `list[str]` | `[]` | Accumulated errors |
### NewsPipeline
`__init__(db_dir: str | Path | None = None)` — Create pipeline. Default dir: `~/.newswatch/`.
`async setup(categories: list[str] | None = None, feeds: list[str] | None = None) -> int` — Subscribe feeds. Returns count added.
`async collect(concurrency: int = 20) -> tuple[int, int]` — Poll feeds. Returns (feeds_ok, new_articles).
`async extract_and_index(limit: int = 50) -> tuple[int, int]` — Fetch content via markgrab (max 10k chars, 15s timeout, >30 words filter), index via embgrep. Returns (extracted, indexed).
`async track_pages(urls: list[str]) -> int` — Monitor URLs for changes via diffgrab. Returns changes count.
`search(query: str, top_k: int = 5) -> list[dict]` — Sync semantic search. Returns dicts with keys: file (str), score (float), text (str, first 200 chars), lines (str).
`async run(extract_limit: int = 50, track_urls: list[str] | None = None) -> PipelineResult` — Full pipeline: collect + extract + index + track. Errors captured, not raised.
`close() -> None` — Close all DB connections.
## Data directory
```
~/.newswatch/
  feeds.db        # feedkit subscriptions (SQLite)
  index.db        # embgrep semantic index (SQLite)
  tracker.db      # diffgrab snapshots (SQLite)
  extracted/      # markgrab markdown files
```
Override: `NewsPipeline(db_dir="/path/to/data")`
## Examples
### Subscribe + run + search
```python
import asyncio
from newswatch.pipeline import NewsPipeline
async def main():
    p = NewsPipeline()
    await p.setup(categories=["technology", "science"])
    result = await p.run(extract_limit=100)
    for hit in p.search("renewable energy", top_k=10):
        print(f"[{hit['score']}] {hit['text']}")
    p.close()
asyncio.run(main())
```
### Step-by-step control
```python
async def steps():
    p = NewsPipeline()
    await p.setup(feeds=["https://hnrss.org/frontpage"])
    feeds_ok, new = await p.collect(concurrency=10)
    extracted, indexed = await p.extract_and_index(limit=25)
    changes = await p.track_pages(["https://news.ycombinator.com"])
    hits = p.search("large language models", top_k=3)
    p.close()
```
### Error handling
```python
result = await pipeline.run()
if result.errors:
    for err in result.errors:
        print(f"Error: {err}")
print(f"{result.feeds_collected} feeds, {result.articles_indexed} indexed")
```
## Links
[Source](https://github.com/QuartzUnit/newswatch) | [PyPI](https://pypi.org/project/newswatch/) | [feedkit](https://github.com/QuartzUnit/feedkit) | [markgrab](https://github.com/QuartzUnit/markgrab) | [embgrep](https://github.com/QuartzUnit/embgrep) | [diffgrab](https://github.com/QuartzUnit/diffgrab)
